signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.12 — Trump rejection of Iran proposal and Middle East tensions sustain USD bid despite normal volatility (VIX 18.4). Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +300bp carry advantage, keeping the pair supported. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation at +0.641 (z=-2.09σ) indicates a significant break from the norm as JPY fails to capture safe-haven flows despite geopolitical risk. Intervention risk remains the primary ceiling as price tests the 160.00 psychological handle.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.79 — G8 bilateral theme active as AUD outperforms JPY by 0.824% on a weekly basis. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 85% short creates a sharp institutional-retail divergence. RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +360bp carry premium, fueling the trend. Retail capitulation risk is elevated if AUD/USD extends gains toward multi-year highs. Watch for BoJ verbal intervention or VIX > 25 to trigger carry unwind.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5962 — COT LF net short -19,294 (18.5% long) shows speculators heavily fading the currency despite a hawkish market bias for the RBNZ. With the RBNZ next meeting on 27 May and a market bias for a hike, a hawkish delivery or signal creates significant short-squeeze risk for crowded speculative shorts. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% currently offers a +150bp carry advantage to USD, but the positioning extreme suggests the downside is exhausted.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme + Technicals
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7771 — Retail sentiment is 87% long, marking a significant contrarian reversal risk as price continues to lean against the 100-hour moving average resistance. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% maintains a +375bp carry advantage for USD, yet the pair remains under pressure (1M: -1.80%). Failure to clear the 100-hour MA confirms retail is catching a falling knife; watch for a break of recent day lows to accelerate the move.
